pending_tickets.entry: ~$207 - approve ANYTIME -> fills at the 7/22 OPEN (multi-day swing, PDT-free, 0/3 day-trades used). Marketable-limit $211 gives a gap-up cushion.
pending_tickets.thesis: Ends a 6th day of live cash by owning the leading AI-infra/semis theme via the NON-extended leader. NVDA +1.9% today LAGGED the +8-12% AMD/MU/INTC complex = a cheaper entry in the group leader; it just took a 9.3% stake in neocloud Nebius and Micron's memory blowout reads straight through to NVDA HBM/GPU demand. No earnings binary until late Aug (AMD 8/4; GOOGL/TSLA print Wed - NVDA doesn't). Wide $186 stop. This IS the gate re-entry - fires at the open (the 7/20 execution-timing fix).

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **The group's biggest laggard, +1.9% while MU/INTC/AMD ran +8-12% — the catch-up name, not a broken one.** Micron's HBM blowout reads straight through to NVDA GPUs; least-extended AI leader, no earnings binary until late Aug (GOOGL/TSLA print Wed, NVDA doesn't). $186 stop under the late-June base. Also the live swing — re-staged to fire at tomorrow's open.
coverage.size_note: Largest paper position; also the live swing pick

projection.basis: laggard catch-up if theme holds; live swing, no own-binary

coverage.hold_reason: This is our core AI position and today's laggard — up ~1.9% while memory names ran +8-12%. We hold 140 sh from ~$208 (about flat) and it just took a 9.3% stake in neocloud Nebius; Micron's HBM-demand blowout reads straight through to NVDA GPUs, so we read it as the catch-up name. It's also the live swing we're trying to own — re-staged to fill at tomorrow's open. The $186 stop is our line if the whole AI trade rolls over.

coverage.name: Advanced Micro Devices
coverage.thesis: **The one leader we actually own into a real move — +7.6% today, and its 'Advancing AI 2026' event is TOMORROW (7/22–23).** Q2 earnings aren't until Aug 4, so no print binary this week — we hold 30 sh through the event on a wide $490 stop rather than trim.

projection.basis: Advancing AI 2026 event 7/22 - same-day catalyst (own it paper)

coverage.hold_reason: AMD is our best-owned leader — up ~7.6% today into its Advancing AI 2026 developer event that starts tomorrow, which is the catalyst driving it (its actual earnings are Aug 4, so no print risk this week). We hold 30 sh from ~$528 and are green ~+$430. We're keeping the stop wide ($490) to ride the event rather than get shaken out, and would only sell if the whole AI trade rolls over.

coverage.name: NuScale Power
coverage.thesis: **Relative-strength leader of the nuclear-for-AI-power group, +8.7%.** Built to 700 sh; volatile small-cap so the stop's wide at $7.50.
coverage.size_note: Doubled up this session into the leader

projection.basis: nuclear RS leader; rides risk-on but extended +9%

coverage.hold_reason: NuScale — small modular reactors, a pure play on the scramble to power AI datacenters. We built up to 700 sh (~$8.63) as it led the nuclear group +9% today. It's a volatile small-cap so the stop's wide at $7.50; we're watching whether the nuclear-for-AI bid follows through or was a one-day pop.

coverage.name: Micron
coverage.thesis: **Today's engine: +12.8% on a blowout — Anthropic multi-year memory deal, BofA reits Buy $1,550 (66% upside), data-center revenue doubling.** The super-cycle read-through we're playing via AMD/NVDA rather than chasing a $977 stock at the high.
coverage.size_note: Not held — too extended/pricey to chase at 2pm

projection.basis: memory leader but very extended +12% - cool-off risk

coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **+16.3% — fuel-cell power-for-AI squeeze, the day's biggest ownable mover.** We correctly aren't chasing it up here; a spent parabolic into Wed earnings-heavy tape is a give-back risk.
coverage.size_note: Not held — extended; watch for a base

projection.basis: spent parabolic +15% - we're OUT, not chasing

coverage.name: Oklo
coverage.thesis: **Started 200 sh mid-day as the nuclear group broke higher, +5.6%.** Basically flat from our entry (caught it after the move); $39 stop under it.

projection.basis: AI-power beta near breakout; tracks the tape

coverage.hold_reason: Another nuclear/AI-power name (Altman-linked); started 200 sh ~$43.92 mid-day as the group broke higher. It's basically flat from our entry — we caught it after the move, honestly — with a $39 stop under it. Watching whether the whole nuclear leg follows through tomorrow or fades.

coverage.name: Rigetti
coverage.thesis: **+7.4% riding the risk-on quantum bid.** Not owned; pure momentum beta with no fresh catalyst of its own.

coverage.thesis: **Datacenter power & cooling, +4.6% — less extended than the chips, which is why we like it here.** Added to 30 sh; $282 stop.

coverage.hold_reason: Vertiv — datacenter power and cooling, the 'picks-and-shovels' of the AI-capex build. Added to 30 sh ~$305; it's less extended than the chips, which is why we like it here. $282 stop; watching the datacenter-capex read-through from this week's mega-cap earnings.

feed.text: 4:15pm FINAL - Grade D+ on the day. +8-15% memory-led rip; paper owned the right theme (AMD/SMR/OKLO/VRT/CEG/NVDA, all 6 GTC-stopped, zero naked) but mid-day entries + a 39%-of-book NVDA anchor (+1.9%) held it to +0.42%, and live sat cash a 6th straight day (0% capture). Saves: no parabolic chase, cut SQQQ/PLTR into the rip, dodged the -16% SOXS trap. Lesson: the gate has no RE-ENTRY - fix staged, NVDA swing fires at tomorrow's open, $186 stop.

3:45pm — Into the close: re-staged the LIVE NVDA swing to fire at TOMORROW's OPEN (approve anytime -> fills 7/22 open, ~$207, $186 stop, 0 day-trades) so it stops dying untapped at 4pm — the 7/20 execution-timing fix. No new paper trade: 6/6 GTC-stopped in the day's leaders (AMD +7.6%, SMR +7.9%, OKLO +5.3%), zero naked, but honest capture ~0-2% on a +8-15% tape -> grade D (running). QQQ $709 still under its $714.7 20-day -> ~$16.4k 3x powder parked for a confirmed reclaim past Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints.

3:11pm — Reconciled both books on fresh data; HOLD, no new trade. 6/6 paper positions GTC-stopped + verified, zero naked; live flat cash (6th day), NVDA swing armed/untapped. Confirmed AMD earnings = Aug 4 (not 7/22) → holding AMD through tomorrow's Advancing AI event on a wide $490 stop. 3x still gated (QQQ $708.7 < $714.7 20-day); ~$16k paper powder dry for the reclaim past Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints. Grade D+ (running): right theme, mid-day entries = tail, live in cash.

2:44pm refresh — HOLD/monitor, no new trade. Book in the leaders, 6/6 GTC-stopped, zero naked. Honest read: capture ~1–2% on a +12–16% day → grade D+ (running). Not chasing MU +12% / BE +15% at 2pm (top-tick risk). 3x still gated (QQQ $709.5 < $714.7 20-day); ~$16k powder for a confirmed reclaim past Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints. LIVE NVDA swing armed + untapped — 6th day in cash.

2:12pm run: HOLD/monitor — no new trade (disciplined, not lazy). Book's in the day's leaders and holding (AMD +7.9%, SMR +8.7%); all 6 paper positions GTC-stopped, zero naked. Not chasing names already +8–16% mid-day — that's how we caught only the tail. 3x gate still shut (QQQ $709.8 vs $714.7 20-day); ~$16k powder dry for a confirmed reclaim, ideally past tomorrow's GOOGL/TSLA prints. LIVE NVDA swing still armed + untapped.

1:42pm run: HOLD/monitor — no new trades (already rotated 4x today into the leaders). Book in the right theme: semis + nuclear/AI-power all green, PAPER 6/6 GTC-stopped, zero naked. Not chasing anything extended up here; the ~$16k 3x powder waits for QQQ to clear its ~$715 20-day. LIVE flat $810 a 6th session (NVDA ticket armed/untapped). Both books == broker.
Sold PLTR — flat-to-red for a week while the AI-infra theme ripped. Took the small loss (−$334) to free the cash for something actually moving.
Started OKLO — nuclear name breaking back above its base with the AI-power bid. $39 stop under it.
Doubled up on SMR — the relative-strength leader of the nuclear group, +9% today. Now 700 shares, $7.50 stop.
Added Vertiv — the data-center power/cooling play, less extended than the chips. Now 30 shares, $282 stop.

12:50pm run: acted on the 'own the movers, gate only the 3x' lesson. Cut the full SQQQ inverse (-5.7% drag) and bought SMR + VRT - the nuclear-AI / data-center movers we'd only watched. Both books reconciled == broker, PAPER 6/6 GTC-stopped zero-naked, LIVE flat $810 (NVDA ticket staged). ~$19k powder armed for a QQQ $715 reclaim.

Bought VRT in paper (18 sh, $282 stop) - the clean AI-infra power/cooling leader, +4.3% and no earnings binary this week (reports 7/29). A higher-quality way to own the data-center buildout than chasing the parabolic BE.

Finally bought SMR (294 sh paper, $7.55 stop) - the nuclear-for-AI mover (+7.7%) we kept watching but not owning. A starter probe to establish real capture on the theme that keeps running (SMR/OKLO/RGTI all green).

Cut the rest of the SQQQ hedge (all 50) - a 3x inverse bleeding -5.7% on a risk-on 2nd day is pure drag on capture with QQQ pushing its 20-day. The stops on the longs bound the downside better than a decaying inverse; redeployed toward leadership + powder.

Added another 25 NVDA in paper (now 140 sh, avg $208.15) - concentrating the non-extended AI leader that LAGGED the chip pop (+1.4% vs peers +5-7%), no earnings binary this week. Re-armed the $186 GTC stop on the full 140.
Trimmed the SQQQ hedge 100->50 - it's dragging -5.5% on a risk-on tape that's reclaiming, so I cut half the inverse (kept the other half as GOOGL/TSLA-Wed insurance) and re-stopped 50 @ $38.

Added 25 NVDA in paper (now 115 sh, avg $208.63) - leaned into the cheap, non-extended AI leader as the chip/AI-infra bid holds a 2nd day. Not chasing +7% INTC into Thursday earnings or the gated +14% SOXL; NVDA's the leader that hasn't run yet.

Trailed the AMD stop up $458->$490 to protect the +6% winner into its 7/22-23 event, and kept the small SQQQ hedge into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints. ~$28k paper powder held dry for a QQQ $716 reclaim (gate still shut at $708).

Staged a LIVE buy on NVDA (3 sh, $186 stop) - the non-extended AI leader (+1.4% vs the +6-14% AMD/INTC/SOXL that are gated or extended into earnings). Approve = fills now; gets live out of a 6th day of cash into the theme that's running.

Retired the live PLTR buy ticket (2026-07-20-3): PLTR is red -1.1% while the AI-infra/chip bid runs - lagging the theme, not leading it. Rotated the live idea into NVDA, the non-extended leader of the group that's bid.

Refreshed the live PLTR buy ticket in place (current levels, $122 stop). Gate shut day 6 (QQQ $705.6 < $716); holding the SQQQ hedge into Wed's earnings wall. No 3x chase.

Refreshed the live PLTR buy ticket (2026-07-20-3) with current levels so it stays tappable and pings fresh - same trade, same $122 stop. It sat a day without a phone ping; this keeps the 1x leader one tap away instead of letting live rot in cash a 6th session.

10:19am management run: both books re-verified clean at the broker (PAPER 5/5 GTC-stopped, zero naked - AMD/PLTR/NVDA/CEG/SQQQ; LIVE flat $810 cash). Trimmed the SQQQ hedge 310->100 sh (~-$244 realized). It was -3.5% and the book's biggest drag on a green tape - stopped camping the losing short, kept 100 sh as insurance into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA/INTC prints. $38 GTC stop on the residual.

Rotated paper toward capture - cut the losing hedge, bought the AMD pop. Live PLTR buy still staged. 3x-index gate still shut (QQQ < $716); no SOXL/TQQQ chase. 0/3 day-trades.

8:19am pre-open: chips ripping again (SOXL +10%, INTC +4.5% on Intel's High-NA EUV first) but NVDA's not confirming (+0.8%) and QQQ ~$705 < $716 20-day -> gate shut day 6, NO 3x chase (faded 5 days running). PLTR live buy staged for the open; paper holds 4/4 incl. the SQQQ hedge into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA/INTC wall. 7:42am pre-open reconcile: fresh pull confirms the plan. QQQ premarket ~$705.7 (+1.38%) still < $716.12 20-day -> gate shut day 6, no 3x chips. PLTR earnings verified Aug 3 (not this week); live buy staged for the open, paper SQQQ hedge held into GOOGL/TSLA Wed. Both books clean, 4/4 GTC-stopped. Day-trade 0/3.

Acted on it: trimmed the SQQQ hedge 100->50 to cut the drag, and added another 25 NVDA (->140 sh) to concentrate the non-extended, no-earnings-binary leader - leaning HARD into 1x leadership while the 3x stays gated (QQQ $708 < $716 20-day). ~$25k paper powder dry to deploy the instant QQQ reclaims $716. Live flat $810, NVDA ticket staged/untapped.
pulse.hype: Still basically flat while the chip names run - and this time they're holding, so the safety rule is costing us, not saving us. Cut half the hedge and bought more of the cheap leader (NVDA); ready to go big the second the Nasdaq clears its line.

pulse.text: 11:52a - the chip/AI-infra bid is HOLDING into midday, not fading like the prior week (SOXL +14%, INTC +7%, AMD +6%); QQQ $708 is still ~1% under its $716 20-day so the leverage gate stays shut day 6 (no SOXL/TQQQ chase). Leaned paper into the cheap non-extended leader - added 25 NVDA (->115 sh) - trailed the AMD winner's stop up to $490, kept the small SQQQ hedge into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints, ~$28k powder dry for a $716 reclaim. Live still flat (NVDA ticket staged, untapped). Honest capture ~0% again: book ~flat while the biggest movers (gated SOXL, binary INTC) run un-ownable - AMD +6% is the one clean capture.
pulse.hype: Chips held up for a 2nd day so I added more of the cheap one (NVDA) and nudged the AMD stop up to lock in the win. Still can't touch the biggest movers - they're the leveraged / earnings-risk names - so we're basically flat while they run.

pulse.text: 11:18am - upgraded the LIVE ticket from the lagging PLTR to NVDA (3 sh ~$206, $186 stop, 0/3 day-trades used): today's leadership is the AI-infra/chip bid (SOX +3.7%, 2nd straight day; NVDA took a stake in neocloud Nebius) and NVDA is the NON-extended leader (+1.4% vs AMD +5.8% / INTC +7.3%), cheapest multiple in ~a decade, no earnings binary this week - a cleaner 'own the theme' entry than chasing the +6-14% names into their prints or holding red PLTR. Gate still SHUT (QQQ $708, -1.1% under its $716 20-day, day 6) so no 3x; holding the paper SQQQ hedge into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA earnings, paper otherwise unchanged (owns pop_rank-2 AMD, 5/5 stopped). Capture still ~0% with the biggest movers gated/extended, but the live setup quality is right and it's staged early, not at 3pm. Approve = NVDA fills now.
pulse.hype: Swapped the live ticket into NVDA - it's the cheap, un-extended one in the chip rally instead of the names that already ran +6-14%. Keeping the little hedge on until Google and Tesla report Wednesday.

pulse.text: 10:54am management run - refreshed the LIVE PLTR buy ticket in place (5 sh, $122 stop, ~$134 marketable) so it's current and pings fresh; it's the 1x RS leader, red today (-0.8%) = a cheaper entry, not a broken thesis, and I'd rather own it than chase INTC +6% / SOXL +12% / AMD +5% into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA and Thu's INTC prints. Gate shut day 6 (QQQ $705.6 still ~1.5% under its $716 20-day), so no 3x chase - SOXL's +12% is the exact setup that's faded 5 sessions straight. Paper holds AMD (owned the pop_rank-1 chip leader since the open) + NVDA/CEG/PLTR + the SQQQ hedge into the earnings wall; I cut the hedge and add the 1x leader only on a QQQ $716 reclaim. Honest capture risk: if the chip bid holds today, our books trail the extended names. Day-trade 0/3.
pulse.hype: Refreshed the live PLTR ticket so it's current - the leader's red today, which is a cheaper entry, not a reason to bail. Still not chasing the chip pop that's faded 5 days running right into this week's big earnings.

pulse.text: 10:19am, one-hour-in management run - no new trades (the morning already did the rotation). Both books re-verified clean: PAPER 5/5 GTC-stopped zero-naked (AMD/PLTR/NVDA/CEG/SQQQ), LIVE flat $810 cash with the PLTR buy (ticket 2026-07-20-3, $122 stop) still staged - one tap fills it now. The open chip pop is fading on cue - SOXL +13%->+9.2%, AMD +6%->+4% (near its day low), QQQ $703 still ~1.8% under its $716 20-day (gate shut day 6) - so no 3x chase. Honest read: paper's -0.5% while the biggest movers run un-owned (BE +10.4% dead-cat, SOXL +9.2% gated, SMR +5.2% spec); AMD's the one clean ownable leader and we hold it. Powder stays dry for a QQQ $716 reclaim or a fade into Wed's GOOGL/TSLA prints. Day-trade 0/3.
pulse.hype: One hour in, nothing worth chasing - the chip pop's fading like it has all week, so no leverage; we own the one clean mover (AMD) while PLTR sits a tap from filling live.

status.text: Leaders held; NVDA staged for open
